**Agrégation avec des données non person: **Aggregation with non-personal data.** We may publish, disclose and use aggregated information — data about all our Users, or about particular groups or categories of Users, combined so that no individual User can be identified — together with non-personal information, for sector and market analysis, demographic profiling, promotional and advertising purposes, and other commercial purposes.
**Agrégation avec des données personnell: **Aggregation with personal data available on the User’s social accounts.** If you connect your account to another service in order to cross-post, that service may pass us your profile and login information, along with anything else you have authorised it to disclose. We may aggregate information about our other Users, groups and accounts with the personal data available about you.
**Aucun traceur de mesure d’audience ou: **No analytics or third-party tracker is placed before you agree to it.** On your first visit, a banner lets you accept everything, refuse everything, or choose category by category. Refusing is as easy as accepting: both buttons are given equal prominence.
**Choix de cookies** — conserve votre dé: **Cookie choice** — keeps your decision so that you are not asked again on every page. Stored in your browser and never sent to our servers. Duration: six months.
**Collecte de l’opt-in (consentement) pr: **Prior consent before any transfer following a merger or acquisition.** Should we take part in a merger, an acquisition or any other transfer of assets, we undertake to obtain your prior consent before transferring your personal data, and to maintain the level of confidentiality to which you consented.
**Collecte des données de profilage et d: **Collection of profiling and technical data in order to provide the service.** Certain technical data about your device is collected automatically by the Site: your IP address, internet service provider, hardware and software configuration, browser type and language, among others. Collecting this data is necessary to provide the services.
**Collecte des données techniques à des: **Collection of technical data for advertising, commercial and statistical purposes.** Technical data about your device is automatically collected and recorded by the Site for advertising, commercial and statistical purposes. It helps us tailor and continually improve your experience on the Site. We neither collect nor keep any personal details — surname, first name, address — that might be attached to a piece of technical data. The data collected may be sold on to third parties.
**Consultation libre** La consultation d: **Free browsing.** Browsing the Site requires neither registration nor prior identification. You may do so without providing any personal details such as your surname, first name or address. We record no personal data for the mere consultation of the Site.
**Depuis le site** — le lien « Gérer mes: **From the site** — the “Manage my cookies” link at the foot of every page brings the banner back so you can change your decision.
**Depuis votre navigateur** — tous les n: **From your browser** — every browser lets you delete existing cookies, block new ones or be warned before each is placed. These settings are found under privacy preferences. Note that blocking all cookies may affect how the site works.
**Durée de conservation des cookies** Co: **How long cookies are kept.** In line with the recommendations of the CNIL, the French data protection authority, cookies are kept for no more than 13 months from the moment they are first placed on the User’s device, and the User’s consent is valid for the same period. The lifetime of a cookie is not extended by each visit, so consent must be renewed once that period has elapsed.
**OpenStreetMap / uMap** — carte des lie: **OpenStreetMap / uMap** — the map of places to visit in Toulouse, on the Tourism page.
**Pas de communication à des tiers** Vos: **No disclosure to third parties.** Your data is not disclosed to any third party. You are however informed that it may be disclosed pursuant to a law or regulation, or by decision of a competent regulatory or judicial authority.
**Préférence de langue** — mémorise si v: **Language preference** — remembers whether you are reading the site in French, English or Spanish. Duration: the browsing session.
**Sécurité des formulaires** — protège l: **Form security** — protects quote requests against automated submissions. Duration: the session.
**Soumis à votre accord.** Certaines pag: **Subject to your agreement.** Some pages embed content hosted by third parties, which may place trackers of their own.
**Soumis à votre accord.** Ils nous indi: **Subject to your agreement.** They tell us which pages are read and where visitors come from, so that we can improve the site. The data is aggregated and cannot identify you.
**Toujours actifs.** Sans eux, le site n: **Always active.** Without them the site cannot work properly. They are not used to track or profile you, and are shared with no one.
**Utilisation de l’identifiant de l’util: **User credentials used only to access the services.** We use your electronic credentials solely for, and for the duration of, the performance of the contract.
**YouTube** (Google Ireland Limited) — v: **YouTube** (Google Ireland Limited) — the lounge bar video. Until you click, only a still image is shown: the player loads only at your request, and no Google cookie is placed before then.

Si Toulouse est aujourd’hui surnommée la: Toulouse owes its nickname, the Pink City, to the Romans. It was during the Pax Romana that the city — then called Palladia Tolosa — inherited the tile and brickwork techniques that still shape its architectural identity. A trading crossroads between the British Isles and the Mediterranean world, it grew rich in temples, ramparts and amphitheatres, some of which can still be seen today, notably the Purpan arena and the collections of the Saint-Raymond museum.
Si vous cherchez que voir à Toulouse en: Start on foot in the historic centre. The Place du Capitole, emblem of the Pink City, is unmissable. Continue to the Basilica of Saint-Sernin, a UNESCO World Heritage site, then to the Saint-Cyprien or Saint-Étienne district as you prefer. A guided or private tour from the tourist office adds depth to the experience.
